summary: Recent updates to Firefox's integration with **WebRTC**, the technology behind video calls, have focused on enhancing video streaming capabilities and overall performance. Notably, support for the H.264 video format has been improved by integrating a more accurate parser and refining how the browser handles media capabilities based on video resolution and frame rate. These changes also include improved logging for video data, which helps developers better diagnose issues, and a more efficient management of audio bitrate for smoother transmissions. Overall, these enhancements are aimed at ensuring a higher quality video communication experience for users, especially as demand for reliable video calls continues to grow.
